channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Women’s Non-Profit Adjusts Operations Amid Soaring Fuel Costs
item.description: A Canadian women’s non-profit organisation has had to make difficult operational decisions due to rising fuel prices. The group, which provides vital community services, relies on transportation for b...

item.title: Air India to reduce and suspend certain international flights amid high fuel costs and airspace restrictions
item.description: Air India has announced that it will temporarily suspend flights on selected international routes from June to August due to record-high jet fuel prices and airspace restrictions in some regions. The ...

item.title: Interior Minister vows to remove illegal constructions and tackle land-grabbing in Islamabad
item.description: Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi has strongly announced that all illegal constructions and activities of land-grabbing mafias in Islamabad will be completely removed. He made these statements during a m...

item.title: DHS Inspector General Audits Warehouse-Based Immigration Detention Program Initiated by Kristi Noem
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security's inspector general has begun an audit of a $38 billion program that converted commercial warehouses into immigration detention centers. The initiative, led by form...

item.title: Air India temporarily suspends services on six international routes due to fuel costs and airspace restrictions
item.description: Air India has announced a temporary reduction in its international flight operations, affecting six overseas routes, including Delhi-Chicago and Mumbai-New York, for the period of June to August 2026....

item.title: House of Representatives sends impeachment articles against VP Duterte to Senate
item.description: The House of Representatives has officially transmitted the articles of impeachment against Vice President Sara Duterte to the Senate. This procedural step marks the next phase in the impeachment proc...

item.title: Former Power Minister Saleh Mamman Sentenced to 75 Years in Prison for N33.8 Billion Fraud
item.description: Na Federal High Court for Abuja wey Justice James Omotosho don sentence former Minister of Power, Saleh Mamman, to 75 years inside prison after e convict am on all 12 counts wey EFCC bring against am ...

item.title: The Rise of Barbra Streisand: Iconic Portraits from the 1960s
item.description: Barbra Streisand, born in 1942, emerged as a groundbreaking singer, actress, and performer during the 1960s. This decade marked her transformation from a teenage nightclub singer to an international s...

item.title: Outdated Witchcraft Law in Malawi Contributes to Violence Amid Rumours
item.description: In May 2026, seven people were tragically killed in southern Malawi following rumours that strangers could make men’s genitals vanish by touch. Medical checks confirmed that no one had actually lost t...

item.title: Tanzania Explores EUR 1.5 Billion Steel Plant Investment with Italy’s Danieli Group in Mtwara
item.description: Tanzania dey for early talks with Italy’s Danieli Group on one big steel plant wey fit cost about EUR 1.5 billion (around TZS 4.2 trillion) for Mtwara. Dis investment aim na to boost local steel produ...

item.title: Montreal Positioned as Leading Candidate for New Defence, Security and Resilience Bank
item.description: Canada, along with international partners, is establishing the Defence, Security and Resilience (DSR) Bank, the first multilateral lender dedicated to the emerging security economy. Christopher Skeete...

item.title: Supreme Court requests government response on Dela Rosa petition, temporary restraining order not granted
item.description: The Supreme Court (SC) of the Philippines has formally requested the national government to provide its comments on a petition filed by former Police Chief Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa concerning an Intern...

item.title: Mexico Reflects on Past World Cups and Prepares for New Challenges
item.description: Mexico is gearing up to host the World Cup, a major event that brings with it both excitement and significant expectations. The country looks back on its previous experiences with the tournament, reca...

item.title: Teen Sports Podcaster Seeks Guidance from Industry Professionals
item.description: A 16-year-old high school junior with a passion for sports broadcasting has started their own podcast in hopes of gaining experience and connections in the field. From the very beginning, the teenager...

item.title: Exploring the 1902 Arrol-Johnston 10/12hp Dogcart: Scotland’s Early Automotive Innovation
item.description: The 1902 Arrol-Johnston 10/12hp Dogcart represents a pioneering achievement in early Scottish automotive history. Created by George Johnston and funded by Sir William Arrol under the Mo-Car Syndicate ...

item.title: Truck Drivers Strike Disrupts Trade at Malaba Border Between Uganda and Kenya
item.description: Earlier this week, truck drivers at the Malaba border launched a strike that seriously disrupted trade between Uganda and Kenya. The protest, sparked by the arrest of Long-Distance Drivers' Union Chai...

item.title: National Assembly Agrees on 31-Member Committee to Consider Ramaphosa Impeachment
item.description: The National Assembly’s Chief Whips Forum has finalised the composition of the Section 89 committee, tasked with determining whether there is prima facie evidence to proceed with impeachment proceedin...

item.title: How Rappler’s Team Covered the Senate Events Amidst Political Turmoil
item.description: During a particularly intense week in Philippine politics, Rappler’s newsroom demonstrated strong teamwork in covering multiple high-stakes events. Reporters Jairo Bolledo and Patrick Cruz, alongside ...

item.title: Media coverage of the 2026 federal budget highlights generational perspectives
item.description: The 2026 federal budget sparked predictable reactions across Australian media, revealing more about the demographic profile of newspaper readers than the policies themselves. Coverage in outlets like ...

item.title: Quebec Duo Popularizes Custom Double-Neck Guitar
item.description: The Quebec prog-rock duo Angine de Poitrine, consisting of musicians Klek and Khn from Saguenay, has captured international attention with their highly original, microtonal music style. Combining elem...
